approach: HITL (mission brief) — this CR narrows a frozen scenario, tripping the CLEARANCE hard floor; emit the verdict packet and STOP at the spec gate, never self-assert past it

Fix the CONTRACT, not the code — blast-estimate's >= 2 work-area guard is already correct and deliberate; the defect is that an engine guard is the de-facto spec while the frozen suite stays silent on the precondition

Option 2 (qualify :123 with the >= 2 precondition) over option 1 (qualify :16) — option 1 is REFUTED not merely dispreferred: it would leave :123 demanding high at size 1 where the engine computes low, making it a code change that flips shipped behavior

Pin the 1-area answer with an ADDITIVE scenario — a decision argued in an engine header but not frozen in a scenario is not specified; this is the edit that makes the contract say what the code does, and it self-clears (stays @frozen, no re-open)
Reconcile the README barrier claim — 'agree by construction' has a hole at size 1, where a barrier computes low; README is prose spec kept in sync, never frozen, so no Clearance
Read the edit class STRUCTURALLY via gherkin-cli diff --format json, never from a raw line diff — prove :123 modified (narrowing) and the 1-area scenario added
Zero engine change and zero existing-test change — both tests already encode the >= 2 precondition (:16 seeds a 3-area corpus commenting the exact contradiction; :123 loops sizes 2/3/4/7, never 1)

why: github-238 (op5-m3) -- the third of Op5's three parts, and the CR where the frozen edit #192 deliberately routed rather than fixed gets ratified. A SPEC defect, not a code defect: blast-estimate.feature's frozen suite was SELF-CONTRADICTORY on a 1-area project. 'a single peripheral work area computes low blast' (one area, no fan-in, no marking -> low) and 'a project-wide touch-set computes high blast' (every work area of its project -> high) bound the SAME input in a corpus holding exactly one work area, demanding opposite answers. No implementation can satisfy both; they are disjoint only if 'a corpus' implies >= 2 work areas, which the suite never said. The engine's >= 2 work-area guard on coverage silently resolved it to low -- deliberate and documented in the engine header, but that made an ENGINE GUARD THE DE-FACTO SPEC while the contract stayed self-contradictory. This CR makes the contract say what the code does. OPTION 2 TAKEN (qualify the project-wide scenario with the >= 2 precondition), and it is REFUTATION of option 1 rather than a churn preference: qualifying the peripheral scenario instead would leave the project-wide one demanding high at size 1 where the engine computes low -- a code change that FLIPS SHIPPED BEHAVIOR, not a contract correction. Option 3 (rule 1-area projects out of scope) declined: the estimator answers correctly at size 1, so there is nothing to exclude. The cold spec-judge strengthened the refutation independently: the old project-wide scenario contradicted THREE scenarios at size 1, not one -- the breaking-semver and surface-location scenarios also assert low on a single-area touch-set -- so option 1 would have required narrowing THREE frozen scenarios where option 2 fixes all three with ONE edit. THREE EDITS: (1) NARROWED the project-wide scenario's Given to 'whose project holds more than one work area' -- the CLEARANCE trip, and the only one; (2) ADDED 'a lone work area is its whole project but is not project-wide reach' pinning size-1 to low with no project named as covered -- ADDITIVE, self-clears, stays @frozen, no re-open, and the LOAD-BEARING edit, since a decision argued in an engine header but not frozen in a scenario is NOT specified; (3) RECONCILED the node README, whose barrier claim ('reaches across its whole project by definition, so its computed blast is high ... agree by construction') had a hole at size 1. Checked whether the barrier's escalation DEPENDS on the estimate before qualifying rather than assuming: it does not -- formation.feature drives the call-out from the Warden's own structural finding and escalates 'regardless of contract impact', and the judge independently disproved a constructed cross-node collision, confirming formation.feature carries no blast reference and formation/README.md disclaims the dependency outright. So the estimate only CORROBORATES; at size 1 the barrier still escalates on reach and only the corroboration is lost -- claim qualified, behavior unchanged. ZERO ENGINE CHANGE and ZERO EXISTING-TEST CHANGE, verified by reading not asserted: both shipped tests ALREADY live under the >= 2 precondition the contract never stated -- the peripheral test seeds a 3-area corpus and comments the exact contradiction ('a 1-area corpus would satisfy both scenarios' Givens at once'), the project-wide test loops sizes 2/3/4/7 never 1, and seedFiller's docstring says its purpose is to keep a corpus 'a real multi-area project rather than the degenerate 1-area shape'. The contract was the ONLY artifact silent on the precondition. The added scenario's Thens were verified against the SHIPPED engine by spike (computed low, projectWide [], count 1, maxFanIn 0), reproduced by the judge on its own harness. Structural edit class via gherkin-cli diff --base origin/main --format json: added 1 / modified 1 / removed 0, the peripheral scenario unchanged. Cold sdd-spec-judge round 1 ALIGNED true (oracle/builder/architect all PASS, no open markers). Its two spec-prose CONTENT_GAPs were fixed BEFORE this gate (README glossary row for 'coverage'/project-wide reach -- the exact distinction the CR turns on, previously prose-only; and a Use Cases row homing the barrier agreement, whose 'every scenario maps to an entry' claim was already false for the project-wide scenario and which this CR widened by one), so the ratified tree is not the tree the judge graded -- both apply its own prescriptions verbatim, the #192 precedent. Its third gap is carried to deliver as a HARD CONDITION: the added scenario has NO test binding and the harness's seedFiller is BUILT to avoid size 1, so a frozen-but-unbound scenario would read UNBOUND and never run -- the CR's own doctrine turned on itself. Mechanical: check-spec-state OK, check-suite OK, concept-index no drift, check-spec-structure no blocking, check-scenario-overlap no duplicates, root pnpm verify 19/20 (the one red is align-spec --check, whose DEFAULT_BASE is HEAD so it flags uncommitted scenario changes -- the guard firing as designed, green after this gate commit stages the .feature). MEDIUM blast; leash auto-none, HITL, CLEARANCE hard floor (frozen-scenario narrowing) -- human-ratified in-session by unional, NEVER self-asserted. Refs #192; this mission closes #238.

github-238 (op5-m3) impl gate. Delivers the contract fix ratified at seq 2 plus its test binding and prose reconcile; the blast-estimate ENGINE ships byte-identical in behavior. Cold sdd-impl-judge round 2 (fresh context, carrying none of round 1): IMPLEMENTATION_PASS true -- all 22 frozen scenarios hand-reasoned and passing on its OWN fixtures (fresh names solo/crowd/pair/duo, multi-project and multi-root layouts, project sizes 1-7). It re-proved the engine diff CODE-IDENTICAL after stripping comments and blanks, confirmed zero existing-test deletions, reproduced both mutations exactly, and independently confirmed NO consumer of projectWide exists outside the node, so the narrowing breaks nothing downstream. ROUND 1 FAILED, and the finding was this CR's own thesis turned on the conductor: a change whose entire point is 'the prose must say what the code does' shipped a FALSE claim in its central reconciled paragraph -- the README asserted UNQUALIFIED that a 1-area project 'computes low', dropping the frozen scenario's own qualifier ('with no fan-in and no sensitive marking'). The judge falsified it against the real engine: a lone area marked sensitive computes medium, one with fan-in 6 computes medium, and one both marked and central computes HIGH -- where the barrier and estimate DO agree, refuting the paragraph's 'the two do not agree' too. The issue's own text carries the same over-claim and the conductor TRANSCRIBED THE BRIEF INSTEAD OF CHECKING IT -- a correlated blind spot inherited from the source, the very failure mode this CR exists to fix in the suite. The conductor had spiked the ENGINE claim but not the PROSE claim. Fixed and verified empirically BEFORE re-judging across four corners; round 2 added a FIFTH (fanIn7+unmarked -> high, centralityScore topping at 3) proving the corrected claim is EXACTLY 'low <=> fanIn==0 AND unmarked'. Round 1's second finding was a genuine builder gap: the 'guard is keyed per PROJECT, not per corpus' claim is one THIS CR newly asserts, yet it was bound by NO test -- mutating the guard to key on touch-set size (resolved.length < 2) passed 41/41 because every fixture is single-project. A CR that asserts a property owes a test for it; added a multi-project fixture, and both mutations now die against exactly the test naming them. Round 2's nine-mutant hunt left five survivors, all pure arithmetic thresholds, correctly judged NOT a defect since the contract fixes ordering properties rather than exact numbers and every ordering property is bound. Its one non-blocking CONTENT_GAP was taken anyway rather than deferred: SKILL.md and the engine header both carried 'the barrier agreement must hold at every project size, not only at 4+', a PRE-EXISTING unqualified universal of the same class that failed round 1, contradicted by this CR's own new text ten lines away in the same file; qualified both to '>= 2'. THE STRUCTURAL FINDING carried to the gate, not hidden: the >= 2 guard the contract now pins was itself COMPLETELY UNPROTECTED before this CR -- removing it fails exactly the one new test while the other 34 suite tests and the live-corpus smoke check (6/6) all pass, the real repo holding no 1-area project. So the engine guard was not merely the de-facto spec, nothing would have caught its deletion. TWO KNOWN LIMITATIONS ratified, not fixed: verify-scenarios reports 0/22 BOUND for this node (junitTestcaseToResult binds only via a spec:<node> suite segment, blast-estimate's tests are flat, and no scenario-bridge.toml exists) -- PRE-EXISTING, identical for the 21 scenarios #192 shipped, so this node's suite is judged wholly by hand every gate; and a 1-area project whose lone area carries cross-project fan-in computes medium, pinned by no scenario (a pre-existing gap, not a contradiction; both judges said do not widen this CR). Both surfaced in the PR as candidate follow-ups rather than auto-filed. Rebased onto origin/main; root pnpm verify 21/21 green; 36 suite tests + 6 smoke; frozen .feature untouched since seq 2. MEDIUM blast; leash auto-none, HITL, CLEARANCE floor -- human-ratified in-session by unional, NEVER self-asserted. Refs #192; this mission closes #238.
